defs: Trial by sample; assay; sample; specimen; smack.
quotes: If those principal works of God . . . be but certain tastes and says, as it were, of that final benefit.
Thy tongue some say of breeding breathes.
He found a sword of better say.

records.ety: [OE. saie, F. saie, fr. L. saga, equiv. to sagum, sagus, a coarse woolen mantle; cf. Gr. sa`gos. See Sagum.]

defs: A kind of silk or satin.
quotes: Thou say, thou serge, nay, thou buckram lord!

defs: A delicate kind of serge, or woolen cloth.
quotes: His garment neither was of silk nor say.

records.ety: [OE. seggen, seyen, siggen, sayen, sayn, AS. secgan; akin to OS. seggian, D. zeggen, LG. seggen, OHG. sagēn, G. sagen, Icel. segja, Sw. säga, Dan. sige, Lith. sakyti; cf. OL. insece tell, relate, Gr. 'e`nnepe (for 'en-sepe), 'e`spete. Cf. Saga, Saw a saying.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Said (sd), contracted from sayed; p. pr. & vb. n. Saying.]

defs: To utter or express in words; to tell; to speak; to declare; as, he said many wise things.
quotes: Arise, and say how thou camest here.

defs: To repeat; to rehearse; to recite; to pronounce; as, to say a lesson.
quotes: Of my instruction hast thou nothing bated In what thou hadst to say?
After which shall be said or sung the following hymn.

defs: To announce as a decision or opinion; to state positively; to assert; hence, to form an opinion upon; to be sure about; to be determined in mind as to.
quotes: But what it is, hard is to say.

defs: To mention or suggest as an estimate, hypothesis, or approximation; hence, to suppose; -- in the imperative, followed sometimes by the subjunctive; as, he had, say fifty thousand dollars; the fox had run, say ten miles.
quotes: Say, for nonpayment that the debt should double, Is twenty hundred kisses such a trouble?

defs: To speak; to express an opinion; to make answer; to reply.
quotes: You have said; but whether wisely or no, let the forest judge.
To this argument we shall soon have said; for what concerns it us to hear a husband divulge his household privacies?

records.ety: [From Say, v. t.; cf. Saw a saying.]

defs: A speech; something said; an expression of opinion; a current story; a maxim or proverb.
quotes: He no sooner said out his say, but up rises a cunning snap.
That strange palmer's boding say, That fell so ominous and drear Full on the object of his fear.
